title: Expose Systemkatalog through a typed read-only operator surface
goal: Let Grabowski query stable ecosystem semantics, truth ownership, relationships and entry points through typed structured calls instead of requiring shell-oriented CLI discovery or ad hoc file parsing.

acceptance.assertion: Typed read-only operations cover system lookup, truth-owner lookup, relationships, entry points, authority matrix and catalog manifest identity.
Every response binds repository commit, registry schema, relevant file digests and generated-manifest identity.
Responses explicitly state that stable semantics do not establish runtime health, task state, pull-request state, CI state or merge readiness.
Missing, malformed, stale-generated or internally inconsistent catalog artifacts produce typed degraded results and do not fall back silently to prose.
Single-repository work does not load the full ecosystem graph unless scope classification requires it; responses are bounded and query-specific.
Contract tests compare typed results with the deterministic Systemkatalog query tool and cover unknown systems, multiple truth owners, cycles, malformed registry and source drift.

metadata.observation: Systemkatalog has strong JSON registries and a deterministic query script, but Grabowski does not expose them as a first-class typed read surface.
